With the innings in danger of folding, Amir Jangoo and Gudakesh Motie dug deep and stitched together a superb 81-run stand that breathed life into the innings. Motie counterattacked with a fluent 41 while Jangoo held one end firm before bringing up a composed half-century, taking West Indies past 170 and raising hopes of a strong finish. But Fisher returned to break the partnership by dismissing both set batters, and that proved to be the decisive blow. The lower order couldn't add much thereafter as Jayden Lennox and Bracewell polished off the tail, with New Zealand wrapping up a dominant bowling show for the third successive time this series. Should be a straightforward chase. Back shortly for reply...

We are back for the run chase. The surface at Kensington Oval is a lot better than the ones we saw in Guyana, and it only got better as the first innings progressed. West Indies will need a few early wickets if they are to make a contest of this and keep the series alive. New Zealand openers Henry Nicholls and Will Young make their way to the middle.
